title: Ensure certificates generated through IBM Cloud Certificate  Manager are automatically renewed before expiration

input_row.title: Ensure certificates generated through IBM Cloud Certificate  Manager are automatically renewed before expiration
input_row.description: You can use the IBM Cloud™ Certificate Manager service dashboard to manage certificates that you obtain from third-party issuers, or order from Certificate Manager, to use with your IBM cloud-based apps or services. You can manually or automatically renew certificates that you order through Certificate Manager. You can also disable the auto-renewal feature after you enable it. You can set a certificate to automatically renew while you're ordering. Or, you can enable auto- renewal after it's configured.
input_row.rationale: Cloud applications and services running on the platform might use certificate for different purposes - TLS or mTLS, client/server authentication and more. Ensuring your certificates are stored securely, and renewed before they expired is crucial to maintaining high security posture.
input_row.audit: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com 2. Click the Menu icon and select Resource List 3. On the Resource List page under Services, perform the following for each Certificate Manager instance that you have provisioned. a. Click on the Certificate Manager instance to view the configuration for the service. b. Click on Your certificates. c. Examine the Expires In column and verify that none of the certificates have expired d. Verify the auto renew toggle is set to on
input_row.remediation: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com 2. Click the Menu icon and select Resource List 3. On the Resource List page under Services, perform the following for each Certificate Manager instance that you have provisioned. a. Click on the Certificate Manager instance to view the configuration for the service. b. In order to renew the certificate manually click on the options menu and select Renew. c. Note certificates manually imported into Certificate Manager cannot be automatically renewed. In order to renew those certificates click the options menu and select Reimport. The process can be automated by configuring Slack or Webhook based expiration notifications in the Notifications section of Certificate Manager Dashboard. From the Certificate Manager instance configuration screen, 1. Click Notifications. 2. Click the Create button. 3. Select the Channel type and enter in the Channel endpoint URL. 4. Ensure the Enable/Disable toggle is set to On. 5. Click the Save icon
input_row.impact: Without proper Certificate Management practices in place organization risk to be vulnerable to impersonation attacks in case certificates used for authentication will be leaked and service outages in case certificates are not renewed on time.
input_row.default_value: By default auto-renew is turned off and certificates must be manually renewed

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_certificate_manager_certificate_auto_renew_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI and Certificate Manager API to check the configuration of certificates.
  
1. **API Calls / CLI Commands:**
   - Utilize the command `ibmcloud certmgr certificate list` to retrieve all certificates from the Certificate Manager.
  
2. **Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Enumerate through all Certificate Manager instances within the IBM Cloud account to verify the configuration.

3. **Specific Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - For each certificate retrieved, check:
     - The `expires_in` property to ensure no certificates are expired.
     - The `auto_renew` property or equivalent setting to confirm that the auto-renew feature is enabled.

4. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - Pass if all certificates are not expired (i.e., `expires_in` is greater than zero) and if the `auto_renew` property is set to true.
   - Fail if any certificate is expired or the `auto_renew` is not set to true.

5. **Example:**
   - After executing the command, verify the output of the `auto_renew` setting for each certificate to ensure compliance with the control.
